title: Topic-feedback sidecar
description: Written by build-topic-feedback.mjs from a project's REAL analytics export (Google Search Console / GA4 CSV) joined to publish-log.jsonl. content-strategy consumes it as EVIDENCE for the next series plan — every suggestion is a proposal that stays unvalidated until keyword-research confirms it with real SERP data. Never fabricated: every metric comes from the export; unknown = null, never 0.

source.description: Where the analytics came from. Mirrors keyword-research's honesty contract.

data_quality.description: measured = real numbers from an export; estimated = inferred. NEVER label a guess 'measured'.

window.description: Date range covered by the export if the user recorded it; null otherwise. Do not fabricate.

inputs.description: The CSV export file(s) this was built from (paths relative to the project).

thresholds.description: The policy thresholds used to classify rows, recorded so the verdicts are reproducible.

published_performance.description: One row per published article (joined from publish-log.jsonl). Metrics are null when the export does not mention the page — null means 'not seen', never 0.

verdict.description: winner = proven clicks; underperformer = seen a lot but ~no clicks (title/intent mismatch); low-data = not enough impressions to judge (incl. not-in-export); neutral = otherwise.

opportunities.description: Queries the site already gets impressions for but does not own (high impressions, low CTR or weak position) and that no published article targets. New-topic candidates — still unvalidated.

suggestions.description: Deterministic, evidence-backed next-action proposals for content-strategy. Proposals only — every one must be confirmed by keyword-research before drafting.

type.description: double-down = make more like a winner; revise = rewrite an underperformer's title/intent; new-topic = pursue an opportunity query.

target.description: The content_id (double-down/revise) or query (new-topic) the suggestion is about.

status.description: Always 'unvalidated' — keyword-research must confirm real demand before any drafting.
